title: Cross-fitted hierarchical target encodings for driver/track strategy memory
summary: The current best stack still relies mostly on numeric heuristics plus frequency encoding.
rationale: The current best stack still relies mostly on numeric heuristics plus frequency encoding. Public encoding guidance shows high-cardinality categories can overfit badly without cross-fitting, while this dataset has many drivers and race/compound combinations with likely stable pit-style signal.
implementation_hint: Build OOF encodings under Race-Year-held-out folds for Driver, Driver x Compound, Driver x Stint, Race x Compound, Race x LapBin, and Driver x RaceProgressBin, with shrinkage/fallback chains. Keep them as a separate specialist model or as additive features to the current booster.
expected_effect: Should capture persistent team/driver/circuit tendencies that raw frequencies miss, with possible CV lift if leakage is fully controlled.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race regime, these encodings will look great locally and fail on leaderboard; unseen drivers/races need strong smoothing fallback.
